objects.title: Risk analysis and information system security
objects.description: Entities must establish and maintain risk management frameworks, perform and document risk assessments, and implement risk treatment plans.

objects.title: Incident handling
objects.description: Establish and implement incident handling policies defining roles, responsibilities, and procedures for detecting, analysing, containing, responding to, and recovering from incidents.

objects.title: Business continuity and crisis management
objects.description: Backup management, disaster recovery, and crisis management procedures to ensure continuity of operations.

objects.title: Supply chain security
objects.description: Security-related aspects of relationships with direct suppliers or service providers, including supply chain risk assessment.

objects.title: Security in acquisition, development and maintenance
objects.description: Security in network and information systems acquisition, development, and maintenance, including vulnerability handling and disclosure.

objects.title: Effectiveness assessment
objects.description: Policies and procedures to assess effectiveness of cybersecurity risk-management measures through audits, testing, and evaluation.

objects.title: Basic cyber hygiene and training
objects.description: Basic cyber hygiene practices and cybersecurity training for personnel.

objects.title: Cryptography and encryption
objects.description: Policies and procedures regarding use of cryptography and encryption to protect data confidentiality and integrity.

objects.title: HR security, access control and asset management
objects.description: Human resources security, access control policies, and asset management to restrict and monitor access to systems and data.

objects.title: MFA and secured communications
objects.description: Multi-factor authentication or continuous authentication, secured voice/video/text communications, and secured emergency communication systems.

objects.title: Reporting obligations
objects.description: Essential and important entities shall notify the CSIRT or competent authority of any significant incident. Reporting follows a phased timeline: early warning, incident notification, intermediate report, and final report.

objects.title: Reporting — Early warning
objects.description: Within 24 hours of becoming aware of a significant incident. Must indicate if suspected unlawful/malicious cause and cross-border impact.

objects.title: Reporting — Incident notification
objects.description: Within 72 hours: update with initial assessment including severity, impact, and indicators of compromise.

objects.title: Reporting — Intermediate report
objects.description: Additional updates on status and handling as requested by CSIRT or competent authority.

objects.title: Reporting — Final report
objects.description: Within 1 month: detailed description, root cause, mitigation measures, and cross-border impact. Progress report if incident ongoing.
